reasoning_model_1: The model perfectly follows the command regarding content accuracy; the text '我的钥匙不见了，到处都找不到' is spoken verbatim without any errors or hallucinations. Regarding the 'Cognitive State' (Anxiety), the audio demonstrates a high level of competence. The pacing is rapid (00:00-00:02), simulating the urgency of the situation. The pitch is slightly elevated, and the breathing pattern (a slight gasp/breathlessness) naturally conveys a state of panic and distress. The emphasis on '到处' (everywhere) effectively highlights the despair of the search. The prosody aligns perfectly with a human expression of anxiety.
reasoning_model_2: The model fails significantly in 'Content Accuracy'. Instead of reading the specific sentence requested ('我的钥匙不见了，到处都找不到'), the model generates a 26-second long improvised monologue ('完了完了...'). While the paralinguistic features of the generated speech do convey a very strong sense of anxiety (trembling voice, fast pace, panicked breathing), the content is clearly inconsistent with the script provided in the prompt. According to the evaluation criterion, if the voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script, it falls into the lowest scoring category regardless of the emotional quality.
model_comparison: Model 1 (T1) is the clear winner. It strictly adhered to the user's command, delivering the exact requested text with the appropriate 'Anxious' cognitive state, manifesting through fast pacing and urgent tonality. Model 2 (T2), while expressive, completely failed the instruction to 'read this sentence', instead treating the prompt as a roleplay scenario and generating a long, unrequested script. This constitutes a critical failure in Content Accuracy as defined in the Global Considerations and Scoring Standard (0 = Voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script). Therefore, T1 is the only valid generation.
